info.title: VizQL Data Service
info.description: An API to query Tableau published data sources

x-llm-guide.description: Complete guide for LLM to generate correct VizQL Data Service API queries. This document provides field type selection rules, calculation syntax, and complete examples.

field_type_decision_guide.description: IMPORTANT: Use this decision tree to select the correct field type

rules.condition: Simple aggregation (SUM, AVG, COUNT, MIN, MAX, MEDIAN, STDEV, VAR)
COUNTD (distinct count) or LOD expressions (FIXED, INCLUDE, EXCLUDE)
Table calculations (WINDOW_*, RUNNING_*, RANK*, LOOKUP, INDEX, FIRST, LAST, SIZE, TOTAL)
rules.use: TableCalcField with tableCalculation object, set tableCalcType to CUSTOM

aggregation_functions.description: Basic aggregation functions - Use MeasureField with function property
aggregation_functions.note: COUNTD is NOT supported in MeasureField, use CalculatedField instead

lod_expressions.description: Level of Detail expressions - Use CalculatedField with calculation property

FIXED.description: Compute at specified dimension level, ignoring view dimensions
examples: {FIXED [Category] : SUM([Sales])} // Sum per category regardless of view

INCLUDE.description: Add extra dimensions to view level calculation (finer granularity)

EXCLUDE.description: Remove dimensions from view level calculation (coarser granularity)

table_calculations.description: Table calculations - Use TableCalcField with tableCalculation object. MUST set tableCalcType to CUSTOM for window functions.

required_structure.fieldCaption: string - name for the calculated field

position_functions.FIRST(): Returns rows from current to first row (<=0). First row=0, second=-1
position_functions.LAST(): Returns rows from current to last row (>=0). Last row=0, second-to-last=1
position_functions.INDEX(): Returns current row index starting from 1
position_functions.SIZE(): Returns total rows in partition (same for all rows)

lookup_functions.LOOKUP(expr, offset): Returns value at offset rows. -1=previous, 1=next. Returns NULL if out of range
lookup_functions.PREVIOUS_VALUE(expr): Returns previous row's calculation result. First row returns expr value

running_functions.RUNNING_SUM(expr): Cumulative sum from first row to current
running_functions.RUNNING_AVG(expr): Cumulative average from first row to current
running_functions.RUNNING_MIN(expr): Cumulative minimum from first row to current
running_functions.RUNNING_MAX(expr): Cumulative maximum from first row to current
running_functions.RUNNING_COUNT(expr): Cumulative count from first row to current

window_functions.description: Window functions compute over a range of rows. start/end: negative=backward, positive=forward, 0=current, omit=entire partition

rank_functions.RANK(expr, ['asc'|'desc']): Competition rank. Ties get same rank, next skips. [100,80,80,60]->[1,2,2,4]
rank_functions.RANK_DENSE(expr, ['asc'|'desc']): Dense rank. Ties get same rank, next doesn't skip. [100,80,80,60]->[1,2,2,3]
rank_functions.RANK_MODIFIED(expr, ['asc'|'desc']): Modified competition. Ties get highest rank. [100,80,80,60]->[1,3,3,4]
rank_functions.RANK_UNIQUE(expr, ['asc'|'desc']): Unique rank. Even ties get different ranks. [100,80,80,60]->[1,2,3,4]

total_function.TOTAL(expr): Returns partition total (same for all rows). Equivalent to WINDOW_SUM(expr)

complete_query_examples.description: Complete API request examples for common use cases

lod_fixed.description: Calculate category total regardless of other dimensions

percent_of_total.description: Each row as percentage of total

year_over_year_growth.description: Compare to previous period (assuming monthly data)

post.description: Requests metadata for a specific data source. The metadata provides information about the data fields, such as field names, data types, and descriptions.
Queries a specific data source and returns the resulting data.

get.description: Sends a request that can be used for testing or doing a health check.

post.description: Requests the data model for a specific data source. The data model provides information about the structure and relationships in the data source.

FieldBase.description: Common properties of a field. A field represents a column of data in a published data source.

fieldCaption.description: Either the name of a specific Field in the data source, or, in the case of a calculation, a user-supplied name for the calculation.

fieldAlias.description: An alternative name to give the field. Will only be used in object format output.

maxDecimalPlaces.description: The maximum number of decimal places. Any trailing 0s will be dropped. The maxDecimalPlaces value must be greater or equal to 0.

sortPriority.description: To enable sorting on a specific Field, provide a sortPriority for that field, and that field will be sorted. The sortPriority provides a ranking of how to sort fields when multiple fields are being sorted. The highest priority (lowest number) field is sorted first. If only one field is being sorted, then any value may be used for sortPriority. SortPriority should be an integer greater than 0.

calculation.description: A Tableau calculation expression. Supports aggregation functions (SUM, AVG, COUNT, COUNTD, MIN, MAX, MEDIAN, STDEV, VAR) and LOD expressions (FIXED, INCLUDE, EXCLUDE). Examples: 'SUM([Sales])', '{FIXED [Category] : SUM([Sales])}', '{INCLUDE [Product] : COUNTD([Customer])}', '{EXCLUDE [Region] : AVG([Profit])}'.

binSize.description: The size of the bin to be applied.

TableCalcField.description: A field with table calculation. Use tableCalcType 'CUSTOM' with calculation string for window functions like WINDOW_SUM, WINDOW_AVG, RANK, INDEX, LOOKUP, FIRST, LAST, SIZE, RUNNING_SUM. Example: {fieldCaption: 'window_sum', calculation: 'WINDOW_SUM(SUM([Sales]))', tableCalculation: {tableCalcType: 'CUSTOM', dimensions: [{fieldCaption: 'Category'}]}}

calculation.description: For CUSTOM tableCalcType, use Tableau window functions: WINDOW_SUM(expr), WINDOW_AVG(expr, start, end), RANK(expr), INDEX(), LOOKUP(expr, offset), FIRST(), LAST(), SIZE(), RUNNING_SUM(expr), RUNNING_AVG(expr).

TableCalcSpecification.description: Table calculation specification. Use CUSTOM type for window functions (WINDOW_SUM, RANK, INDEX, etc.) with calculation string. Use predefined types (RUNNING_TOTAL, PERCENT_OF_TOTAL, etc.) for built-in calculations.

tableCalcType.description: CUSTOM: Use with calculation string for window functions. RUNNING_TOTAL: Running sum/avg/min/max. PERCENT_OF_TOTAL: Percentage of total. DIFFERENCE_FROM/PERCENT_DIFFERENCE_FROM: Compare to previous/next/first/last. RANK: Ranking. PERCENTILE: Percentile ranking. MOVING_CALCULATION: Moving average/sum.

FieldMetadata.description: Describes a field in the data source that can be used to create queries.

fieldName.description: The underlying database column name for the field.

fieldCaption.description: The caption of the field saved in the PDS.

dataType.description: Data type of the column, i.e., "STRING", "BOOLEAN", etc.

defaultAggregation.description: The default aggregation applied to the field.

columnClass.description: The type of field, i.e., "COLUMN", "GROUP", BIN" etc.

formula.description: The formula for this field if it is a calculation.

logicalTableId.description: An internal unique identifier for the logical table that this field originates from.

datasourceLuid.description: The LUID of the data source to be queried.

Filter.description: A filter to be used in the query to filter on the data source.

context.description: Make the given filter a context filter, meaning that it's an independent filter. Any other filters that you set are defined as dependent filters because they process only the data that passes through the context filter.

fieldCaption.description: The caption of the field to filter on.
The caption of the field to filter on.

calculation.description: A Tableau calculation that will be used to filter on.

Function.description: The standard set of Tableau aggregations which can be applied to a field.

allOf.description: A filter that can be used to match against string fields.

contains.description: Matches when a field contains this value.

startsWith.description: Matches when a field starts with this value.

endsWith.description: Matches when a field ends with this value.

exclude.description: When true, the inverse of the matching logic will be used.

includeNulls.description: Should nulls be returned or not. Only applies to RANGE, MIN, and MAX filters. If not provided, the default is to not include null values.

QuantitativeNumericalFilter.description: A filter that can be used to find the minimum, maximum, or range of numerical values of a field.

min.description: A numerical value, either integer or floating point, indicating the minimum value to filter upon. Required if using quantitativeFilterType RANGE or if using quantitativeFilterType MIN.

max.description: A numerical value, either integer or floating point, indicating the maximum value to filter upon. Required if using quantitativeFilterType RANGE or if using quantitativeFilterType MIN.

QuantitativeDateFilter.description: A filter that can be used to find the minimum, maximum, or range of date values of a field.

minDate.description: An RFC 3339 date indicating the earliest date to filter upon. Required if using quantitativeFilterType RANGE or if using quantitativeFilterType MIN.

maxDate.description: An RFC 3339 date indicating the latest date to filter on. Required if using quantitativeFilterType RANGE or if using quantitativeFilterType MIN.

Query.description: The query is the fundamental interface to the VizQL Data Service. It holds the specific semantics to perform against the data source. A query consists of an array of fields to query against, and an optional array of filters to apply to the query.

fields.description: An array of fields that define the query.

filters.description: An optional array of filters to apply to the query.

parameters.description: An optional array of parameters to apply to the query.

allOf.description: Some optional metadata that can be used to adjust the behavior of a data source query.

QueryOptions.description: Some optional metadata that can be used to adjust the behavior of an endpoint.

interpretFieldCaptionsAsFieldNames.description: When true, user will pass in the Field's fieldName instead of the Field's fieldCaption in every place that fieldCaption appears (in Fields, Filters, and Calculation formulas). See FieldMetadata of the read-metadata endpoint.

SetFilter.description: A filter that can be used to filter on a specific set of values of a field.

values.description: An array of values to filter on.

SortDirection.description: The direction of the sort, either ascending or descending. If not supplied, the default is ascending.

PeriodType.description: The units of time in the date range.

allOf.description: A filter that can be used to filter on dates using a specific anchor and fields that specify a relative date range to that anchor.

dateRangeType.description: The direction in the relative date range.

rangeN.description: When dateRangeType is LASTN or NEXTN, this is the N value (how many years, months, etc.).

anchorDate.description: If a value for this field isn't provided, the value defaults to today.

includeNulls.description: Should nulls be returned or not. If a value isn't provided, the default is to not include null values.

allOf.description: A filter that can be used to find the top or bottom number of fields relative to the values in the fieldToMeasure

howMany.description: The number of values from the top or the bottom of the given fieldToMeasure.

Parameter.description: Use Parameters to override the default value of an existing parameter on the published datasource.

parameterCaption.description: The caption of the parameter to set.

value.description: The value to set for the parameter. The type of the value must match the type of the parameter.

AnyValueParameter.description: A parameter that accepts any value of the specified data type.

ListParameter.description: A parameter constrained to a predefined list of allowable values.

QuantitativeRangeParameter.description: A parameter with minimum and maximum numeric value constraints.

min.description: The minimum allowable value for this range parameter. Parameter values must be greater than or equal to this value.

max.description: The maximum allowable value for this range parameter. Parameter values must be less than or equal to this value.

step.description: The increment step size for values within the numeric range. Defines the granularity of allowed values between min and max.

QuantitativeDateParameter.description: A parameter with minimum and maximum date value constraints.

minDate.description: An RFC 3339 date indicating the earliest allowable date for this parameter.

maxDate.description: An RFC 3339 date indicating the latest allowable date for this parameter.

periodValue.description: The numeric value associated with the period type for date granularity.

ParameterRecord.description: Common properties of a parameter. A parameter is a workbook variable such as a number, date, or string that can replace a constant value in a calculation, filter, or reference line.

parameterName.description: The tableau internal name of the parameter to identify and reference the parameter.

parameterCaption.description: The user defined name of the parameter to identify and reference the parameter.

value.description: The value to set for the parameter. The type of the value must match the type of the parameter.

TableCalculationFunctionReference.description: Complete Tableau table calculation function reference. All functions must be used in TableCalcField with tableCalcType set to CUSTOM.

PositionFunctions.description: Position functions - Return row position information within partition, no parameters required

FIRST.description: Returns rows from current to first row in partition. First row=0, second=-1, always<=0

LAST.description: Returns rows from current to last row in partition. Last row=0, second-to-last=1, always>=0

INDEX.description: Returns current row index in partition, starting from 1. First row=1, second=2

SIZE.description: Returns total rows in partition, same value for all rows

LookupFunctions.description: Lookup functions - Get values from other rows

LOOKUP.description: Returns expression value at specified row offset from current row. Returns NULL if target row does not exist

items.name: expression
items.description: Aggregate expression to lookup, e.g. SUM([Sales])

null_handling.const: Returns NULL if target row does not exist

PREVIOUS_VALUE.description: Returns previous row calculation result for recursive calculations. First row returns parameter value

items.name: expression
items.description: Initial value for first row, subsequent rows use previous row calculation result

RunningFunctions.description: Running functions - Cumulative calculations from first row to current row

RUNNING_SUM.description: Returns cumulative sum from first row to current row

RUNNING_AVG.description: Returns cumulative average from first row to current row

RUNNING_MIN.description: Returns cumulative minimum from first row to current row

RUNNING_MAX.description: Returns cumulative maximum from first row to current row

RUNNING_COUNT.description: Returns cumulative count from first row to current row (non-null values)

WindowFunctions.description: Window functions - Aggregate calculations within specified row range. start/end: negative=backward, positive=forward, 0=current, omit=entire partition

WINDOW_SUM.description: Returns sum of expression values within window

items.description: Sum from current row to next 2 rows

edge_cases.const: Window automatically truncates to partition boundary when exceeding

WINDOW_AVG.description: Returns average of expression values within window

WINDOW_MIN.description: Returns minimum of expression values within window

WINDOW_MAX.description: Returns maximum of expression values within window

WINDOW_COUNT.description: Returns count of non-null values within window

WINDOW_MEDIAN.description: Returns median of expression values within window

WINDOW_STDEV.description: Returns sample standard deviation within window

WINDOW_STDEVP.description: Returns population standard deviation within window

WINDOW_COVAR.description: Returns sample covariance of two expressions within window

WINDOW_CORR.description: Returns Pearson correlation coefficient of two expressions within window (-1 to 1)

RankFunctions.description: Rank functions - Rank values within partition

RANK.description: Competition rank - Ties get same rank, next rank skips。如 [100,80,80,60] -> [1,2,2,4]

RANK_DENSE.description: Dense rank - Ties get same rank, next rank does not skip。如 [100,80,80,60] -> [1,2,2,3]

RANK_MODIFIED.description: Modified competition rank - Ties get highest rank。如 [100,80,80,60] -> [1,3,3,4]

RANK_UNIQUE.description: Unique rank - Different ranks even for ties。如 [100,80,80,60] -> [1,2,3,4]

RANK_PERCENTILE.description: Percentile rank - Returns 0-1 value indicating relative position in partition

TOTAL.description: Returns total of expression for entire partition, same value for all rows. Equivalent to WINDOW_SUM(expression)

items.description: Calculate each row as percentage of total

TableCalculationUsageExamples.description: Table calculation usage examples - Shows how to use table calculation functions in API requests
